features.name: Mechanical Alarm Trigger
features.rationale: Table-stakes per NFPA 101, IBC Chapter 10. Safety (8): 95 dB deterrent addresses N10 OS=14. Reliability (8): mechanical fail-safe at 0V. Confidence (8): eliminates dead-alarm scenario.

features.name: Key Override Mechanism
features.rationale: Mandatory for operational use — facilities maintenance, lab managers, security staff require authorized silent access daily. Stress (7): eliminates alarm-trigger anxiety during routine authorized access.

features.name: Pre-Alarm Warning Tone
features.rationale: Absent from basic prop alarms. Campus environments have high incidental door-contact rates. Stress (8): dramatically reduces alarm desensitization risk. Pain (7): eliminates alarm disruption. Delight (7): genuinely unexpected.

features.name: Wireless State Transmitter
features.rationale: More reliable coverage proportionally eliminates more patrol burden. Time (8): real-time alert replaces patrol delay. Stress (8): overnight security officer confidence. Reliability capped at 5: C3 30m limit creates dead zones.

features.name: Battery Power Supply
features.rationale: Defining characteristic of this commodity class. >75% of campus buildings exceed 40 years old with no wiring to secondary exits. Cost (9): eliminates electrician labor. Skill (8): facilities crew installs without trades.

features.name: Remote Battery Status Reporting
features.rationale: Zero remote battery visibility in all mechanical alternatives. Stress (8): eliminates overnight anxiety about dead batteries at dormitory exits. Pain (8): replaces manual battery-check rounds. Confidence (8): proactive replacement enables Clery Act coverage continuity.

features.name: Retrofit Surface Mount Installation
features.rationale: More retrofit-friendly = proportionally lower deployment cost. Cost (9): zero electrician cost. Skill (9): facilities technician installs in <30 min/door. Pain (9): removes dominant adoption barrier for legacy-building campuses.

features.name: Automated Event Log Generation
features.rationale: Highest scoring feature. Clery Act compliance documentation is top underserved need cluster (N18 OS=15, N19 OS=15). Stress (9): eliminates pre-inspection documentation scramble. Pain (9): replaces manual compilation. Confidence (9): 12-month digital audit trail on demand.

productRelatedNeeds: Minimize the likelihood of wireless signal attenuation in reinforced-concrete campus buildings exceeding the 30 m EasyWave transmitter range
Minimize the likelihood of coverage gaps discovered only after campus-wide deployment requiring unplanned repeater infrastructure
Minimize the likelihood of IoT battery exhaustion on dormitory exits where 24/7 student occupancy depletes batteries before the 2-year maintenance cycle
Minimize the integration effort to relay EasyWave alarm events into campus PSIM or BAS standardized on BACnet, KNX, or IP-based protocols
Minimize FERPA or GDPR compliance exposure from door-event timestamps attributable to resident students in dormitory buildings
Minimize the likelihood of surface-mounted exit alarm housings being tampered with by students in unsupervised dormitory corridors
